cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking why the writer tells visitors and drivers to look around when traveling. The options are A to D. First, I need to look back at the article. The last paragraph says: "The next time you visit a park or drive through an area with a lot of wildlife, look around. You might see an animals overpass!" So the reason given here is that you might see an animals overpass, which is an ecopassage. The ecopassages are the bridges and underpasses mentioned earlier in the text. The answer should relate to seeing these structures used by animals. Looking at the options: A) Wild animals may attack cars – The article doesn't mention attacks, just accidents from hitting animals. So probably not A. B) Wild animals may jam the road – The problem discussed is animals being hit by cars, not them blocking roads. So B seems unlikely. C) They may see wild animals in the park – While the park is mentioned, the specific reason given in the text is about ecopassages, not just animals in the park.
